content.content:
1	# Paper Scan Processor
2	
3	A desktop application for post-processing PDF files containing batches of scanned documents. The app has two modes: **Merge** and **Split**.
4	
5	## Tech stack
6	
7	- **Desktop framework**: Wails
8	- **Backend**: Go
9	- **Frontend**: TypeScript + React
10	- **UI components**: Mantine + `@mantine/dropzone`
11	- **Virtualization**: TanStack Virtual
12	- **Drag-and-drop**: dnd-kit
13	- **State**: Zustand
14	- **PDF manipulation**: pdfcpu
15	- **PDF rendering** (thumbnails): `mutool` (system install, called as subprocess)
16	
17	## Common elements
18	
19	Both modes include a thumbnail panel and a page detail panel, and support the same per-page editing operations.
20	
21	### Thumbnail panel
22	
23	A vertically scrolling strip of page thumbnails. Width is user-adjustable via a drag handle; thumbnails scale to fill the panel width. Rendering is on-demand and virtualized вҖ” only visible thumbnails (plus a small overscan buffer) are rendered at any given time.
24	
25	The Go backend exposes a per-page render method returning a base64-encoded PNG; the frontend requests thumbnails as they scroll into view (`mutool draw` subprocess).
26	
27	#### Keyboard shortcuts
28	
29	| Key                    | Action                                     |
30	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
31	| `R`                    | Rotate selected page clockwise 90В°         |
32	| `Shift+R`              | Rotate selected page counter-clockwise 90В° |
33	| `Delete` / `Backspace` | Toggle skip on the selected page           |
34	| `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’`              | Select previous / next page                |
35	
36	### Page detail panel
37	
38	Shows the currently selected page at reading resolution. Selecting a thumbnail updates it.
39	
40	Supports:
41	
42	- **Pan**: drag to pan.
43	- **Zoom**: scroll wheel or trackpad pinch to zoom in/out.
44	- **Navigate**: `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’` to move to the previous/next page.
45	
46	Implemented with `react-zoom-pan-pinch`.
47	
48	### Page editing
49	
50	Individual pages can be edited in both modes before export or merge:
51	
52	- **Rotation**: pages can be rotated in 90В° increments (clockwise or counter-clockwise).
53	- **Skip**: pages can be marked as skipped вҖ” excluded from the output but remaining visible in the thumbnail view (greyed out). A page is skipped by clicking a skip icon that appears on hover, or via keyboard shortcut.
54	- **Reorder**: pages can be reordered by dragging thumbnails to a new position.
55	
56	## Mode: Merge
57	
58	For scanners that can only scan one side at a time. The user scans all front pages as one PDF and all back pages as another, then uses Merge mode to interleave them into a single PDF.
59	
60	### Workflow
61	
62	1. The user loads two PDF files вҖ” one containing front pages, one containing back pages.
63	2. The user selects which file contains the first page (i.e. designates which is "fronts" and which is "backs").
64	3. A **Reverse back pages** checkbox controls whether the back PDF is reversed before interleaving. This should be checked when the paper stack was flipped between scans (the typical case), causing backs to be in reverse order.
65	4. The app interleaves the pages: front 1, back 1, front 2, back 2, etc.
66	5. The user saves the merged result as a new PDF file on disk.
67	
68	The merged PDF can then be opened in Split mode for further processing.
69	
70	### Unequal page counts
71	
72	If the front and back PDFs have different page counts, the app shows a warning before proceeding: "Front has X pages, back has Y pages. The extra Z page(s) will be appended at the end." The user can cancel or continue. The extra pages from the longer file are appended in order after the interleaved section.
73	
74	### Layout
75	
76	Merge mode uses a two-column layout:
77	
78	- **Left panel** вҖ” two side-by-side thumbnail strips, one per input file.
79	- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view, showing whichever page was most recently selected in either thumbnail strip.
80	
81	The thumbnail strip for the file containing the second output page is offset downward by half a thumbnail height. This makes the interleaving order visually apparent: the fronts and backs appear to slot between each other.
82	
83	```
84	  Fronts          Backs
85	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
86	  в”Ӯ front 1  в”Ӯ
87	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
88	                в”Ӯ  back 1  в”Ӯ
89	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
90	  в”Ӯ front 2  в”Ӯ
91	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
92	                в”Ӯ  back 2  в”Ӯ
93	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
94	  в”Ӯ front 3  в”Ӯ
95	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
96	```
97	
98	The "Fronts" / "Backs" labels above each strip reflect the current **First page is in** selection and update when it changes.
99	
100	### Error handling
101	
102	TBD.
103	
104	## Mode: Split
105	
106	### Workflow
107	
108	1. The user loads an input PDF via a file picker dialog or by dragging and dropping a file onto the app window.
109	2. The app displays all pages as thumbnails in the left panel. Clicking a thumbnail selects it and updates the detail panel on the right.
110	3. The user defines split points by clicking in the gaps between page thumbnails. A visual divider appears at each split point; clicking again removes it. Dividers can also be repositioned by dragging them to a different gap. Each divider marks where a new output file begins.
111	4. The user sets a global filename template using `{date}` (today's date as `YYYY-MM-DD`) and `{name}` (a per-file label). Example: `{date} {name}` вҶ’ `2026-06-12 invoice.pdf`. The app prefills the filename for each output file using this template. The user can then edit any individual file's name before exporting.
112	5. The user sets a global output folder (an existing folder on the local filesystem, or a Google Drive folder if Drive integration is enabled). For each output file, the user can adjust:
113	   - The prefilled filename (editable)
114	   - The destination folder (overridable per file)
115	6. The user clicks Export. Before splitting, the app checks for filename conflicts at each destination. If any conflict is found, the export is aborted and an error message identifies the conflicting files. Once resolved, the app splits the input PDF and writes (or uploads) each output file. Afterwards, the app prompts the user to keep, move, or delete the input file.
116	
117	### Layout
118	
119	Split mode uses a two-column layout:
120	
121	- **Left panel** (adjustable width, drag handle on right edge) вҖ” a single vertically scrolling area that combines the thumbnail strip and output file controls.
122	- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view.
123	
124	#### Left panel structure
125	
126	The left panel is a continuous scroll area. Pages are grouped by output file. Each group is preceded by a compact **output file header** containing the editable filename field and (when applicable) destination folder. Split-point dividers between groups are the visual boundary between one output file and the next.
127	
128	```
129	в”Ңв”Җ invoice.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
130	в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
131	в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
132	  [page 1 thumbnail]
133	  [page 2 thumbnail]
134	  [page 3 thumbnail]
135	  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [gap / split point] в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
136	в”Ңв”Җ receipt.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
137	в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
138	в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
139	  [page 4 thumbnail]
140	  [page 5 thumbnail]
141	```
142	
143	Clicking a gap toggles a split point there and creates a new output file section. The filename and folder fields for each section appear immediately above its pages.
144	
145	### Keyboard shortcuts
146	
147	| Key     | Action                                                  |
148	| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
149	| `Space` | Toggle a split point after the selected page            |
150	| `Tab`   | Move focus to the next filename input in the left panel |
151	
152	### Google Drive integration (optional)
153	
154	- Authentication uses Google OAuth via a browser window, triggered the first time Drive is enabled. Credentials are stored locally and reused in future sessions.
155	- When Drive is enabled, each output file header in the left panel shows a Drive folder field. Clicking it opens a folder browser modal that displays the user's Drive folder tree. The user navigates the tree and selects a destination folder. The selected path is shown in the header (e.g. `My Drive / Clients / Smith / 2026`).
156	- The folder tree is fetched lazily вҖ” the first time the user opens the folder browser in a session.
157	- The folder browser shows a **recently used folders** list at the top for quick access. This list persists across sessions.
158	- The global output folder setting can be set to a Drive folder, which prefills all output file headers. Individual headers can be overridden.
159	- After export, each output file is uploaded to its designated Drive folder. If an upload fails, the affected header shows an inline error message and a Retry button; other uploads are unaffected.
160	- When Drive is enabled, the local subfolder name for each output file is derived automatically from the Drive folder path (see Local folder sorting below).
161	
162	### Local folder sorting
163	
164	Each output file can be assigned a subfolder name. The file is then saved to `[output folder] / [subfolder] / filename.pdf` rather than directly into the output folder.
165	
166	When Drive is enabled, the subfolder name is derived automatically from the innermost component of the Drive folder path (e.g. `My Drive / Clients / Smith / 2026` вҶ’ subfolder `2026`). When Drive is disabled, the user enters the subfolder name manually in the left panel header.
167	
168	If two or more output files in the same batch share the same subfolder name, the app prefixes the parent folder name to disambiguate. If the parent name is also shared, it walks further up the hierarchy until uniqueness is achieved. Examples:
169	
170	- `Clients / Smith / 2026` and `Clients / Jones / 2026` вҶ’ `Smith - 2026` and `Jones - 2026`
171	- `Clients / Smith / 2026` and `Archive / Smith / 2026` вҶ’ `Clients - Smith - 2026` and `Archive - Smith - 2026`
172	
173	(When Drive is disabled and names are entered manually, the user is responsible for avoiding conflicts вҖ” no automatic disambiguation applies.)
174	
175	To ensure disambiguation is fully deterministic, **the output folder must be empty when the input PDF is opened**. The app enforces this and shows an error if the folder is not empty.
176	
177	### Persisted settings
178	
179	The following settings are saved across sessions:
180	
181	- Last-used output folder
182	- Filename template
183	
184	### Error handling
185	
186	TBD.
187	
188	## Implementation checklist
189	
190	### Primitives
191	
192	- [x] **Go: PDF merge/split** вҖ” interleave and split PDFs with pdfcpu; hardcoded paths; `_test.go` harness, no UI
193	- [x] **Frontend: Thumbnail panel** вҖ” virtualized vertical scroll, on-demand per-page render via Go/mutool, resizable width with drag handle
194	- [x] **Frontend: Detail panel** вҖ” pan and zoom with `react-zoom-pan-pinch`, driven by selected page
195	
196	### Merge mode
197	
198	- [ ] **Go: file dialogs** вҖ” `OpenFileDialog` / `SaveFileDialog` on the App struct (reused by Split)
199	- [ ] **Go: MergePDFs** вҖ” backend method wiring pdfcpu interleave logic to Wails RPC
200	- [ ] **Frontend: Merge UI** вҖ” file pickers, fronts/backs selector, reverse checkbox, page-count mismatch warning, Merge & Save button
201	- [ ] **Frontend: Merge layout** вҖ” two offset thumbnail strips + detail panel
202	- [ ] **Page editing in Merge** вҖ” rotate, skip, reorder
203	
204	### Split mode
205	
206	- [ ] **Go: render + split** вҖ” per-page PNG render and PDF split/export backend methods
207	- [ ] **Frontend: Split layout** вҖ” resizable left panel + detail panel
208	- [ ] **Frontend: Split points** вҖ” click gaps to toggle, drag to reposition, visual dividers
209	- [ ] **Frontend: Output file headers** вҖ” filename template, prefill, per-file folder override
210	- [ ] **Frontend: Export flow** вҖ” conflict check, keep/move/delete prompt after export
211	- [ ] **Page editing in Split** вҖ” rotate, skip, reorder
212	- [ ] **Keyboard shortcuts**
213	
214	### Google Drive integration
215	
216	- [ ] **OAuth authentication** вҖ” browser-based sign-in, credentials stored locally and reused across sessions
217	- [ ] **Folder browser modal** вҖ” lazy-loaded Drive folder tree, opens when clicking a folder field
218	- [ ] **Recently used folders** вҖ” shown at the top of the folder browser, persisted across sessions
219	- [ ] **Upload on export** вҖ” per-file upload to designated Drive folder, inline error + Retry on failure
220	- [ ] **Local subfolder derivation** вҖ” subfolder name derived automatically from the innermost Drive path component; disambiguation when multiple files share the same leaf name
221	
222	### Polish
223	
224	- [ ] **Persisted settings** вҖ” last-used output folder, filename template
225	
226	## Future / out of scope for v1
227	
228	- **Insert pages from another PDF**: allow the user to pull pages from a second PDF into the current document before splitting. Planned for v2.
229	- **Scanning integration**: trigger a scan from within the app using OS or device APIs. Not currently planned but under consideration.

# Paper Scan Processor

A desktop application for post-processing PDF files containing batches of scanned documents. The app has two modes: **Merge** and **Split**.

## Tech stack

- **Desktop framework**: Wails
- **Backend**: Go
- **Frontend**: TypeScript + React
- **UI components**: Mantine + `@mantine/dropzone`
- **Virtualization**: TanStack Virtual
- **Drag-and-drop**: dnd-kit
- **State**: Zustand
- **PDF manipulation**: pdfcpu
- **PDF rendering** (thumbnails): `mutool` (system install, called as subprocess)

## Common elements

Both modes include a thumbnail panel and a page detail panel, and support the same per-page editing operations.

### Thumbnail panel

A vertically scrolling strip of page thumbnails. Width is user-adjustable via a drag handle; thumbnails scale to fill the panel width. Rendering is on-demand and virtualized вҖ” only visible thumbnails (plus a small overscan buffer) are rendered at any given time.

The Go backend exposes a per-page render method returning a base64-encoded PNG; the frontend requests thumbnails as they scroll into view (`mutool draw` subprocess).

#### Keyboard shortcuts

| Key                    | Action                                     |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `R`                    | Rotate selected page clockwise 90В°         |
| `Shift+R`              | Rotate selected page counter-clockwise 90В° |
| `Delete` / `Backspace` | Toggle skip on the selected page           |
| `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’`              | Select previous / next page                |

### Page detail panel

Shows the currently selected page at reading resolution. Selecting a thumbnail updates it.

Supports:

- **Pan**: drag to pan.
- **Zoom**: scroll wheel or trackpad pinch to zoom in/out.
- **Navigate**: `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’` to move to the previous/next page.

Implemented with `react-zoom-pan-pinch`.

### Page editing

Individual pages can be edited in both modes before export or merge:

- **Rotation**: pages can be rotated in 90В° increments (clockwise or counter-clockwise).
- **Skip**: pages can be marked as skipped вҖ” excluded from the output but remaining visible in the thumbnail view (greyed out). A page is skipped by clicking a skip icon that appears on hover, or via keyboard shortcut.
- **Reorder**: pages can be reordered by dragging thumbnails to a new position.

## Mode: Merge

For scanners that can only scan one side at a time. The user scans all front pages as one PDF and all back pages as another, then uses Merge mode to interleave them into a single PDF.

### Workflow

1. The user loads two PDF files вҖ” one containing front pages, one containing back pages.
2. The user selects which file contains the first page (i.e. designates which is "fronts" and which is "backs").
3. A **Reverse back pages** checkbox controls whether the back PDF is reversed before interleaving. This should be checked when the paper stack was flipped between scans (the typical case), causing backs to be in reverse order.
4. The app interleaves the pages: front 1, back 1, front 2, back 2, etc.
5. The user saves the merged result as a new PDF file on disk.

The merged PDF can then be opened in Split mode for further processing.

### Unequal page counts

If the front and back PDFs have different page counts, the app shows a warning before proceeding: "Front has X pages, back has Y pages. The extra Z page(s) will be appended at the end." The user can cancel or continue. The extra pages from the longer file are appended in order after the interleaved section.

### Layout

Merge mode uses a two-column layout:

- **Left panel** вҖ” two side-by-side thumbnail strips, one per input file.
- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view, showing whichever page was most recently selected in either thumbnail strip.

The thumbnail strip for the file containing the second output page is offset downward by half a thumbnail height. This makes the interleaving order visually apparent: the fronts and backs appear to slot between each other.

```
  Fronts          Backs
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ front 1  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                в”Ӯ  back 1  в”Ӯ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  в”Ӯ front 2  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                в”Ӯ  back 2  в”Ӯ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  в”Ӯ front 3  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

The "Fronts" / "Backs" labels above each strip reflect the current **First page is in** selection and update when it changes.

### Error handling

TBD.

## Mode: Split

### Workflow

1. The user loads an input PDF via a file picker dialog or by dragging and dropping a file onto the app window.
2. The app displays all pages as thumbnails in the left panel. Clicking a thumbnail selects it and updates the detail panel on the right.
3. The user defines split points by clicking in the gaps between page thumbnails. A visual divider appears at each split point; clicking again removes it. Dividers can also be repositioned by dragging them to a different gap. Each divider marks where a new output file begins.
4. The user sets a global filename template using `{date}` (today's date as `YYYY-MM-DD`) and `{name}` (a per-file label). Example: `{date} {name}` вҶ’ `2026-06-12 invoice.pdf`. The app prefills the filename for each output file using this template. The user can then edit any individual file's name before exporting.
5. The user sets a global output folder (an existing folder on the local filesystem, or a Google Drive folder if Drive integration is enabled). For each output file, the user can adjust:
   - The prefilled filename (editable)
   - The destination folder (overridable per file)
6. The user clicks Export. Before splitting, the app checks for filename conflicts at each destination. If any conflict is found, the export is aborted and an error message identifies the conflicting files. Once resolved, the app splits the input PDF and writes (or uploads) each output file. Afterwards, the app prompts the user to keep, move, or delete the input file.

### Layout

Split mode uses a two-column layout:

- **Left panel** (adjustable width, drag handle on right edge) вҖ” a single vertically scrolling area that combines the thumbnail strip and output file controls.
- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view.

#### Left panel structure

The left panel is a continuous scroll area. Pages are grouped by output file. Each group is preceded by a compact **output file header** containing the editable filename field and (when applicable) destination folder. Split-point dividers between groups are the visual boundary between one output file and the next.

```
в”Ңв”Җ invoice.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  [page 1 thumbnail]
  [page 2 thumbnail]
  [page 3 thumbnail]
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [gap / split point] в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
в”Ңв”Җ receipt.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  [page 4 thumbnail]
  [page 5 thumbnail]
```

Clicking a gap toggles a split point there and creates a new output file section. The filename and folder fields for each section appear immediately above its pages.

### Keyboard shortcuts

| Key     | Action                                                  |
| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Space` | Toggle a split point after the selected page            |
| `Tab`   | Move focus to the next filename input in the left panel |

### Google Drive integration (optional)

- Authentication uses Google OAuth via a browser window, triggered the first time Drive is enabled. Credentials are stored locally and reused in future sessions.
- When Drive is enabled, each output file header in the left panel shows a Drive folder field. Clicking it opens a folder browser modal that displays the user's Drive folder tree. The user navigates the tree and selects a destination folder. The selected path is shown in the header (e.g. `My Drive / Clients / Smith / 2026`).
- The folder tree is fetched lazily вҖ” the first time the user opens the folder browser in a session.
- The folder browser shows a **recently used folders** list at the top for quick access. This list persists across sessions.
- The global output folder setting can be set to a Drive folder, which prefills all output file headers. Individual headers can be overridden.
- After export, each output file is uploaded to its designated Drive folder. If an upload fails, the affected header shows an inline error message and a Retry button; other uploads are unaffected.
- When Drive is enabled, the local subfolder name for each output file is derived automatically from the Drive folder path (see Local folder sorting below).

### Local folder sorting

Each output file can be assigned a subfolder name. The file is then saved to `[output folder] / [subfolder] / filename.pdf` rather than directly into the output folder.

When Drive is enabled, the subfolder name is derived automatically from the innermost component of the Drive folder path (e.g. `My Drive / Clients / Smith / 2026` вҶ’ subfolder `2026`). When Drive is disabled, the user enters the subfolder name manually in the left panel header.

If two or more output files in the same batch share the same subfolder name, the app prefixes the parent folder name to disambiguate. If the parent name is also shared, it walks further up the hierarchy until uniqueness is achieved. Examples:

- `Clients / Smith / 2026` and `Clients / Jones / 2026` вҶ’ `Smith - 2026` and `Jones - 2026`
- `Clients / Smith / 2026` and `Archive / Smith / 2026` вҶ’ `Clients - Smith - 2026` and `Archive - Smith - 2026`

(When Drive is disabled and names are entered manually, the user is responsible for avoiding conflicts вҖ” no automatic disambiguation applies.)

To ensure disambiguation is fully deterministic, **the output folder must be empty when the input PDF is opened**. The app enforces this and shows an error if the folder is not empty.

### Persisted settings

The following settings are saved across sessions:

- Last-used output folder
- Filename template

### Error handling

TBD.

## Implementation checklist

### Primitives

- [x] **Go: PDF merge/split** вҖ” interleave and split PDFs with pdfcpu; hardcoded paths; `_test.go` harness, no UI
- [x] **Frontend: Thumbnail panel** вҖ” virtualized vertical scroll, on-demand per-page render via Go/mutool, resizable width with drag handle
- [x] **Frontend: Detail panel** вҖ” pan and zoom with `react-zoom-pan-pinch`, driven by selected page

### Merge mode

- [ ] **Go: file dialogs** вҖ” `OpenFileDialog` / `SaveFileDialog` on the App struct (reused by Split)
- [ ] **Go: MergePDFs** вҖ” backend method wiring pdfcpu interleave logic to Wails RPC
- [ ] **Frontend: Merge UI** вҖ” file pickers, fronts/backs selector, reverse checkbox, page-count mismatch warning, Merge & Save button
- [ ] **Frontend: Merge layout** вҖ” two offset thumbnail strips + detail panel
- [ ] **Page editing in Merge** вҖ” rotate, skip, reorder

### Split mode

- [ ] **Go: render + split** вҖ” per-page PNG render and PDF split/export backend methods
- [ ] **Frontend: Split layout** вҖ” resizable left panel + detail panel
- [ ] **Frontend: Split points** вҖ” click gaps to toggle, drag to reposition, visual dividers
- [ ] **Frontend: Output file headers** вҖ” filename template, prefill, per-file folder override
- [ ] **Frontend: Export flow** вҖ” conflict check, keep/move/delete prompt after export
- [ ] **Page editing in Split** вҖ” rotate, skip, reorder
- [ ] **Keyboard shortcuts**

### Google Drive integration

- [ ] **OAuth authentication** вҖ” browser-based sign-in, credentials stored locally and reused across sessions
- [ ] **Folder browser modal** вҖ” lazy-loaded Drive folder tree, opens when clicking a folder field
- [ ] **Recently used folders** вҖ” shown at the top of the folder browser, persisted across sessions
- [ ] **Upload on export** вҖ” per-file upload to designated Drive folder, inline error + Retry on failure
- [ ] **Local subfolder derivation** вҖ” subfolder name derived automatically from the innermost Drive path component; disambiguation when multiple files share the same leaf name

### Polish

- [ ] **Persisted settings** вҖ” last-used output folder, filename template

## Future / out of scope for v1

- **Insert pages from another PDF**: allow the user to pull pages from a second PDF into the current document before splitting. Planned for v2.
- **Scanning integration**: trigger a scan from within the app using OS or device APIs. Not currently planned but under consideration.

message.content: Propose a plan for implementing merge mode. Don't split work by files, but start with the most basic workflow than slowly add features.

content.text:
Here's a progression from bare pipeline to full feature set:

---

**Step 1: End-to-end merge pipeline (no previews)**

Wire up Go file dialogs (`OpenFileDialog`, `SaveFileDialog`) and `MergePDFs` as Wails RPCs. Minimal UI: two "Choose file" buttons showing the selected path, a "Merge & Save" button. No thumbnails. Proves the core pipeline works before building any UI on top of it.

---

**Step 2: Thumbnail strips**

Add the two-column layout using the existing `ThumbnailPanel` primitive, one per file. No offset yet вҖ” just both strips side by side, loading on demand. The Merge & Save button moves to a toolbar or header.

---

**Step 3: Fronts/backs selector + visual offset**

Add the "First page is in" toggle. Strip labels ("Fronts" / "Backs") reflect the selection. Offset the second strip down by half a thumbnail height to make the interleaving visually apparent.

---

**Step 4: Reverse checkbox + page-count mismatch warning**

Add the "Reverse back pages" checkbox (wired into the merge call). Detect unequal page counts before merging and show the confirmation modal ("extra Z page(s) will be appended").

---

**Step 5: Detail panel**

Wire in the existing `DetailPanel` primitive on the right. Selection in either thumbnail strip updates it. Keyboard navigation (`вҶҗ`/`вҶ’`) works across both strips.

---

**Step 6: Page editing**

Rotate, skip, and reorder within the merge view вҖ” applied before the merge call.

27	func (a *App) startup(ctx context.Context) {
28		a.ctx = ctx
29	}
30	
31	// OpenPDF shows a file-open dialog filtered to PDFs and returns the selected path.
32	// Returns an empty string if the user cancels.
33	func (a *App) OpenPDF() (string, error) {
34		path, err := runtime.OpenFileDialog(a.ctx, runtime.OpenDialogOptions{
35			Title: "Open PDF",
36			Filters: []runtime.FileFilter{
37				{DisplayName: "PDF Files (*.pdf)", Pattern: "*.pdf"},
38			},
39		})
40		return path, err
41	}
42	
43	// PageCount returns the number of pages in the PDF at path.
44	func (a *App) PageCount(path string) (int, error) {
45		return pdfPageCount(path)
46	}
47	
48	// RenderPage renders a single page of the PDF at path as a PNG and returns it
49	// base64-encoded. widthPx controls the output width; height is scaled proportionally.
50	func (a *App) RenderPage(path string, pageNum int, widthPx int) (string, error) {
51		tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "psp-render-*")
52		if err != nil {
53			return "", err
54		}
55		defer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)
56	
57		outPrefix := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "page")
58		page := strconv.Itoa(pageNum)
59	
60		out, err := exec.Command(
61			"pdftoppm",
62			"-f", page,
63			"-l", page,
64			"-singlefile",
65			"-png",
66			"-scale-to-x", strconv.Itoa(widthPx),
67			"-scale-to-y", "-1",
68			path,
69			outPrefix,
70		).CombinedOutput()
71		if err != nil {
72			return "", fmt.Errorf("pdftoppm: %w\n%s", err, out)
73		}
74	
75		data, err := os.ReadFile(outPrefix + ".png")
76		if err != nil {
77			return "", err
78		}
79		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(data), nil
80	}

import { useState } from 'react'
import { Box, Group, Tabs, Text } from '@mantine/core'
import MergeMode from './components/MergeMode'
import SplitMode from './components/SplitMode'

type AppMode = 'split' | 'merge'

export default function App() {
  const [mode, setMode] = useState<AppMode>('split')

  return (
    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', height: '100vh', overflow: 'hidden' }}>
      <Box
        component="header"
        style={{
          height: 48,
          flexShrink: 0,
          borderBottom: '1px solid var(--mantine-color-gray-3)',
          display: 'flex',
          alignItems: 'center',
          paddingInline: 'var(--mantine-spacing-md)',
        }}
      >
        <Group justify="space-between" style={{ width: '100%' }}>
          <Text fw={600} size="sm">Paper Scan Processor</Text>
          <Tabs value={mode} onChange={(v) => v && setMode(v as AppMode)}>
            <Tabs.List>
              <Tabs.Tab value="split">Split</Tabs.Tab>
              <Tabs.Tab value="merge">Merge</Tabs.Tab>
            </Tabs.List>
          </Tabs>
        </Group>
      </Box>
      <Box style={{ flex: 1, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
        {mode === 'split' ? <SplitMode /> : <MergeMode />}
      </Box>
    </Box>
  )
}

7	export default function SplitMode() {
8	  const [pdfPath, setPdfPath] = useState<string | null>(null)
9	  const [pageCount, setPageCount] = useState(0)
10	  const [selectedPage, setSelectedPage] = useState(1)
11	
12	  const handleOpen = async () => {
13	    const path = await OpenPDF()
14	    if (!path) return
15	    const count = await PageCount(path)
16	    setPdfPath(path)
17	    setPageCount(count)
18	    setSelectedPage(1)
19	  }
20	
21	  return (
22	    <Box style={{ display: 'flex', height: '100%' }}>
23	      {pdfPath ? (
24	        <ThumbnailPanel
25	          pdfPath={pdfPath}
26	          pageCount={pageCount}
27	          selectedPage={selectedPage}
28	          onSelectPage={setSelectedPage}
29	        />
30	      ) : null}
31	
32	      {pdfPath ? (
33	        <DetailPanel
34	          pdfPath={pdfPath}
35	          pageNum={selectedPage}
36	          pageCount={pageCount}
37	          onNavigate={setSelectedPage}
38	        />
39	      ) : (
40	        <Box style={{ flex: 1, display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center' }}>
41	          <Center>
42	            <Button onClick={handleOpen}>Open PDF</Button>
43	          </Center>
44	        </Box>
45	      )}
46	    </Box>
47	  )
48	}

1	# Paper Scan Processor
2	
3	A desktop application for post-processing PDF files containing batches of scanned documents. The app has two modes: **Merge** and **Split**.
4	
5	## Tech stack
6	
7	- **Desktop framework**: Wails
8	- **Backend**: Go
9	- **Frontend**: TypeScript + React
10	- **UI components**: Mantine + `@mantine/dropzone`
11	- **Virtualization**: TanStack Virtual
12	- **Drag-and-drop**: dnd-kit
13	- **State**: Zustand
14	- **PDF manipulation**: pdfcpu
15	- **PDF rendering** (thumbnails): `mutool` (system install, called as subprocess)
16	
17	## Common elements
18	
19	Both modes include a thumbnail panel and a page detail panel, and support the same per-page editing operations.
20	
21	### Thumbnail panel
22	
23	A vertically scrolling strip of page thumbnails. Width is user-adjustable via a drag handle; thumbnails scale to fill the panel width. Rendering is on-demand and virtualized вҖ” only visible thumbnails (plus a small overscan buffer) are rendered at any given time.
24	
25	The Go backend exposes a per-page render method returning a base64-encoded PNG; the frontend requests thumbnails as they scroll into view (`mutool draw` subprocess).
26	
27	#### Keyboard shortcuts
28	
29	| Key                    | Action                                     |
30	| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
31	| `R`                    | Rotate selected page clockwise 90В°         |
32	| `Shift+R`              | Rotate selected page counter-clockwise 90В° |
33	| `Delete` / `Backspace` | Toggle skip on the selected page           |
34	| `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’`              | Select previous / next page                |
35	
36	### Page detail panel
37	
38	Shows the currently selected page at reading resolution. Selecting a thumbnail updates it.
39	
40	Supports:
41	
42	- **Pan**: drag to pan.
43	- **Zoom**: scroll wheel or trackpad pinch to zoom in/out.
44	- **Navigate**: `вҶҗ` / `вҶ’` to move to the previous/next page.
45	
46	Implemented with `react-zoom-pan-pinch`.
47	
48	### Page editing
49	
50	Individual pages can be edited in both modes before export or merge:
51	
52	- **Rotation**: pages can be rotated in 90В° increments (clockwise or counter-clockwise).
53	- **Skip**: pages can be marked as skipped вҖ” excluded from the output but remaining visible in the thumbnail view (greyed out). A page is skipped by clicking a skip icon that appears on hover, or via keyboard shortcut.
54	- **Reorder**: pages can be reordered by dragging thumbnails to a new position.
55	
56	## Mode: Merge
57	
58	For scanners that can only scan one side at a time. The user scans all front pages as one PDF and all back pages as another, then uses Merge mode to interleave them into a single PDF.
59	
60	### Workflow
61	
62	1. The user loads two PDF files вҖ” one containing front pages, one containing back pages.
63	2. The user selects which file contains the first page (i.e. designates which is "fronts" and which is "backs").
64	3. A **Reverse back pages** checkbox controls whether the back PDF is reversed before interleaving. This should be checked when the paper stack was flipped between scans (the typical case), causing backs to be in reverse order.
65	4. The app interleaves the pages: front 1, back 1, front 2, back 2, etc.
66	5. The user saves the merged result as a new PDF file on disk.
67	
68	The merged PDF can then be opened in Split mode for further processing.
69	
70	### Unequal page counts
71	
72	If the front and back PDFs have different page counts, the app shows a warning before proceeding: "Front has X pages, back has Y pages. The extra Z page(s) will be appended at the end." The user can cancel or continue. The extra pages from the longer file are appended in order after the interleaved section.
73	
74	### Layout
75	
76	Merge mode uses a two-column layout:
77	
78	- **Left panel** вҖ” two side-by-side thumbnail strips, one per input file.
79	- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view, showing whichever page was most recently selected in either thumbnail strip.
80	
81	The thumbnail strip for the file containing the second output page is offset downward by half a thumbnail height. This makes the interleaving order visually apparent: the fronts and backs appear to slot between each other.
82	
83	```
84	  Fronts          Backs
85	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
86	  в”Ӯ front 1  в”Ӯ
87	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
88	                в”Ӯ  back 1  в”Ӯ
89	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
90	  в”Ӯ front 2  в”Ӯ
91	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
92	                в”Ӯ  back 2  в”Ӯ
93	  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
94	  в”Ӯ front 3  в”Ӯ
95	  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
96	```
97	
98	The "Fronts" / "Backs" labels above each strip reflect the current **First page is in** selection and update when it changes.
99	
100	### Error handling
101	
102	TBD.
103	
104	## Mode: Split
105	
106	### Workflow
107	
108	1. The user loads an input PDF via a file picker dialog or by dragging and dropping a file onto the app window.
109	2. The app displays all pages as thumbnails in the left panel. Clicking a thumbnail selects it and updates the detail panel on the right.
110	3. The user defines split points by clicking in the gaps between page thumbnails. A visual divider appears at each split point; clicking again removes it. Dividers can also be repositioned by dragging them to a different gap. Each divider marks where a new output file begins.
111	4. The user sets a global filename template using `{date}` (today's date as `YYYY-MM-DD`) and `{name}` (a per-file label). Example: `{date} {name}` вҶ’ `2026-06-12 invoice.pdf`. The app prefills the filename for each output file using this template. The user can then edit any individual file's name before exporting.
112	5. The user sets a global output folder (an existing folder on the local filesystem, or a Google Drive folder if Drive integration is enabled). For each output file, the user can adjust:
113	   - The prefilled filename (editable)
114	   - The destination folder (overridable per file)
115	6. The user clicks Export. Before splitting, the app checks for filename conflicts at each destination. If any conflict is found, the export is aborted and an error message identifies the conflicting files. Once resolved, the app splits the input PDF and writes (or uploads) each output file. Afterwards, the app prompts the user to keep, move, or delete the input file.
116	
117	### Layout
118	
119	Split mode uses a two-column layout:
120	
121	- **Left panel** (adjustable width, drag handle on right edge) вҖ” a single vertically scrolling area that combines the thumbnail strip and output file controls.
122	- **Right panel** (fills remaining space) вҖ” the page detail view.
123	
124	#### Left panel structure
125	
126	The left panel is a continuous scroll area. Pages are grouped by output file. Each group is preceded by a compact **output file header** containing the editable filename field and (when applicable) destination folder. Split-point dividers between groups are the visual boundary between one output file and the next.
127	
128	```
129	в”Ңв”Җ invoice.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
130	в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
131	в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
132	  [page 1 thumbnail]
133	  [page 2 thumbnail]
134	  [page 3 thumbnail]
135	  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [gap / split point] в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
136	в”Ңв”Җ receipt.pdf в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
137	в”Ӯ folder: /Documents                 в”Ӯ
138	в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
139	  [page 4 thumbnail]
140	  [page 5 thumbnail]
141	```
142	
143	Clicking a gap toggles a split point there and creates a new output file section. The filename and folder fields for each section appear immediately above its pages.
144	
145	### Keyboard shortcuts
146	
147	| Key     | Action                                                  |
148	| ------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
149	| `Space` | Toggle a split point after the selected page            |
150	| `Tab`   | Move focus to the next filename input in the left panel |
151
